enrichedRequirements.productRequest: Build a simple booking system where users can create bookings, choose a date and time, enter customer details, cancel bookings, filter upcoming and past bookings, and see the next upcoming booking. It should work in the browser.
enrichedRequirements.enrichedPrompt:
Build a simple booking system where users can create bookings, choose a date and time, enter customer details, cancel bookings, filter upcoming and past bookings, and see the next upcoming booking. It should work in the browser.

--- Proof scenario clarification answers ---
Business: Browser-based appointment booking platform for scheduling for founders and small teams with clear value proposition and target market.
Users: Customers and staff users who create and manage bookings in the browser.
Roles: Single end-user role for booking operators; no separate admin portal for MVP.
Permissions: Users have full CRUD permissions and write access on bookings they create with read access to schedules.
Workflows: Core workflow process covers create booking, date/time selection stages, customer details capture, cancel booking, upcoming/past filter, next upcoming booking.
Data: Booking entity with id, date, time, customer details, and status fields stored in client state records
Files: No file upload or document attachments required for MVP.
Notifications: No email, SMS, push, alerts, or reminder notifications in MVP.
Integrations: No email integration, calendar sync, or third-party API partner required; product does not integrate with external systems for MVP.
AI: No AI, LLM, chatbot, or recommendation features in MVP.
Monetization: Free productivity tool with no subscription, billing, or payments.
Deployment: Static Vite React SPA for public launch in modern browsers; npm build produces dist/index.html for cloud hosting.
Lead management: No separate lead management pipeline — contact follow-up workflow process only for MVP.
Sales pipeline: No sales pipeline stages beyond follow-up status tracking for MVP scope.
